figure.title: UALink TL Transmit Path Block Diagram
figure.purpose: Details the internal components and data flow for the transmit (Tx) path within the UALink Transaction Layer.

technical_summary: Takes inputs from the external UPLI Originator and Completer.
Contains internal queues: Rsp Queue and Req Queue.
Contains a Tx Address Cache to optimize address transmission.
Contains a large buffer for Data, Write/Atomic BE, and Atomic Operands.
Data from queues and buffers go into a Packing Arbitration Mux, then into an 8-Sector Packing Buffer.
The output is a formatted 64-Byte Outbound Transmit (Tx) TL Flit.

components.name: Tx Address Cache
components.description: Caches addresses to compress transaction headers.

components.name: Packing Arbitration Mux
components.description: Selects and arbitrates data to be packed into the flit.

components.name: 8-Sector Packing Buffer
components.description: Buffer that holds the assembled 64-byte flit divided into 8 sectors.

ordered_flow: Queues/Buffers -> Packing Arbitration Mux -> 8-Sector Packing Buffer -> TL Flit

critical_facts: The TL transmit path involves a packing arbitration mux that organizes transactions into an 8-sector (64-byte) buffer before transmission.
